raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased airline tickets from XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year> using my American Express card for a total of {$4000.00}. On XX/XX/year>, my family and I arrived at the airport for our flight to XXXX. A XXXX XXXX agent wrongfully denied us boarding, claiming we lacked proper travel documents. This was incorrectU.S. citizens ( myself and my child ) do not need a visa for XXXX, and my wife, a green card holder, is eligible for a visa on arrival. 

After a delay of over 2.5 hours, the agent admitted the mistake and issued our boarding passes, but by then, we had already missed the flight. I disputed the charge with Amex and initially received a partial credit of {$3000.00}. 

XXXX XXXX first claimed I didnt have proper documents. After I provided proofincluding boarding passes and passportsAmex did not re-engage with the merchant and closed the dispute, rebilling me {$960.00}. They later claimed the merchant provided documentation justifying the charge, but the original justification was already proven false with my documents. 

I did not receive the service I paid for, and Amex failed to fairly evaluate my evidence. I am requesting a refund for the remaining {$960.00}. 

The case reference with American express is XXXX
